Seeding Reference Data

After running migrations, Merlin's database is structurally correct but empty. Several pieces of reference data must exist before the application is usable: roles, a chart of accounts, payment terms, and an initial administrator account. Merlin ships with five seeders that create this data.

Why seed

Merlin's permission system, chart of accounts, and payment term calculations all depend on reference rows that cannot be inferred from the application code alone. Without these rows, users cannot log in, invoices have nowhere to post to, and due-date calculations have no terms to reference. Seeding is a required step after a fresh installation, not an optional one.

Available seeders

There are five seeders, each responsible for one area of reference data:

Roles and permissions

RolesAndPermissionsSeeder creates the Administrator role. Permissions themselves are created by database migrations (not by seeders), so they already exist by the time this seeder runs. The seeder simply ensures the Administrator role exists in the roles table so it can be assigned to users.

Note: an accountant role is also created by the permissions migrations (specifically the migration that adds the access-panel permission). That role is not created by this seeder.

Chart of accounts

ChartOfAccountsSeeder seeds the account type hierarchy (Asset, Liability, Equity, Income/Revenue, Expense) and a default general ledger chart covering account codes from the 1000s through the 5000s. This gives you a working chart of accounts from day one. You can add, rename, or deactivate accounts through the UI after installation. See Chart of Accounts.

Default admin user

DefaultAdminUserSeeder creates the initial administrator user with the email admin@example.com and the password changeme, and assigns it the Administrator role. Change the password immediately after first login. Do not use these credentials in a production environment.

Debtor account group

DebtorAccountGroupSeeder creates the accounts-receivable account group used by the billing module. This group must exist before sales invoices can be created, because the billing module expects a debtor account group to be present when resolving receivable accounts.

Payment terms

PaymentTermSeeder seeds a set of default payment terms (for example, Net 30, EOM) that can be attached to suppliers, clients, and invoices. You can add or edit terms through the UI at any time. See Payment Terms.

Running all seeders

Running php artisan db:seed without a class argument executes the DatabaseSeeder, which calls all five seeders in the correct order:

php artisan db:seed

To run a specific seeder individually:

php artisan db:seed --class=RolesAndPermissionsSeeder
php artisan db:seed --class=ChartOfAccountsSeeder
php artisan db:seed --class=DefaultAdminUserSeeder
php artisan db:seed --class=DebtorAccountGroupSeeder
php artisan db:seed --class=PaymentTermSeeder

Re-running seeders safely

All five seeders use firstOrCreate or equivalent logic, so re-running them will not create duplicate records or overwrite existing data. You can safely re-run any seeder on a database that is already in use — for example, after pulling a new version of the application that adds reference data to a seeder.

Never run php artisan migrate:fresh or truncate tables on a production database. Migrations are forward-only (they have no rollback methods) and a fresh migration would destroy all existing data.
